title: Chinese Models And The US AI Boom
text:
Chinese Models And The US AI Boom

Will Denyer

Gavekal Research | 23 Jun 2026

With the launch of its GLM-5.2 model, Z.ai has mounted the most impressive Chinese challenge yet to US dominance in artificial intelligence. According to Artificial Analysis, the new model from Beijing-based and Hong Kong-listed Z.ai (formerly Zhipu AI) almost matches the latest models from Anthropic and OpenAI, and outperforms Google’s top model. At the same time, Microsoft is considering adding models from China’s DeepSeek to its Copilot offerings. If it does, it will be betting that many consumers will favor cheap good-enough models over more expensive leading-edge products.Together, these two developments confirm that Chinese AI is now a front-line competitor. The implications for US and global equities are far reaching. Consider the following groups of companies:
